title: Mobavenue AI Tech reports Rs 218 crore FY26 revenue, expands operations across UK and LATAM
description: New Delhi: Mobavenue AI Tech announced its audited consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended 31st March 2026. Growth during the year was supported by strong direct advertiser demand across structurally expanding sectors, including Quick Commerce, BFSI, Fintech, Retail and Emerging segments, alongside continued momentum across omnichannel formats such as Connected TV (CTV), mobile, video and audience intelligence-led platforms. The Company expanded its strategic presence across the UK and LATAM markets, completed the 100% acquisition of Mobavenue Media Pvt. Ltd. and advanced its proprietary AI infrastructure across the A3 framework (Awareness, Acquisition and Activation). During the year, the Company also completed its first preferential capital raise of Rs. 4,999.99 Lakhs to support technology investments, strategic expansion and acquisition opportunities, introduced the ESOP 2025 scheme, and approved a sub-division of equity shares as part of broader organisational and governance initiatives. During FY2026, the company expanded its global presence with the launch of operations in the UK, giving it access to European agency holding groups, while also strengthening its presence in Latin America across mobile-first digital markets. International business contributed 11.5% to overall revenue, with the company now serving more than 150 brands across 10 countries. Direct clients accounted for 73.9% of FY2026 revenue, reflecting stronger enterprise relationships and lower dependence on intermediaries. The company also completed the 100% acquisition of Mobavenue Media to strengthen its AI-powered advertising and consumer growth ecosystem. During the year, it delivered 42.72 million outcomes annually, reaching around 2.5 billion devices globally. Revenue per outcome increased to Rs 48.44 in Q4 FY2026, compared to Rs 47.45 in Q3, Rs 45.89 in Q2 and Rs 44.99 in Q1. The company was also recognised with a Gold award at the AdTech Honours by AdTech India in the Programmatic & Emerging Media and AI & Creative Automation categories, and was featured in Aerospike’s ‘Champions of Scale 2025’. The company strengthened its AI and technology infrastructure during the year by transitioning its core decision-making engine to a proprietary neural network modelling framework. The system now delivers real-time inference in under 15 milliseconds and processes more than 125 crore consented and privacy-compliant consumer and campaign signals daily across devices, audiences and digital touchpoints. Its proprietary GMP 360 platform stack continued to operate without dependence on third-party DSPs. The ecosystem includes PrsmX for omnichannel awareness, SurgeX for user acquisition, DiscvrX and AmplifiX for OEM and app discovery, and ResurgeX for re-engagement and retention. The company also introduced a central AI workbench integrating deep neural networks, transformer models, predictive and statistical modelling, bid optimisation, audience segmentation, fraud detection and real-time learning capabilities. The company further moved towards near-hourly machine learning model refresh cycles, enabling closed-loop optimisation across bids, creatives, targeting and inventory, while remaining aligned with global data protection standards. During the year, it also launched AI-driven campaign planning tools, automated inventory and audience recommendations, conversational LLM-based reporting and a unified single sign-on system across its platform ecosystem. The company said its AI-led capabilities delivered measurable results for clients. Snabbit recorded a five-fold increase in installs and a 10-fold rise in first-time purchases, while Goibibo achieved a two-times improvement in click-to-install efficiency. Sebamed reported a 1.27% click-through rate with 110% delivery through targeted life-stage campaigns. Under its Mission 2030 strategy, the company plans to expand its presence in the UK and Latin America while entering additional emerging and developed markets through direct teams, agency and reseller partnerships, and selective acquisitions. The company said it will continue to follow an asset-light and outcome-based business model as part of its global expansion plans. The company also plans to increase investments in its AI Centre of Excellence and AI Labs, while expanding purpose-built platforms across areas such as Streaming TV, digital out-of-home (DOOH), retail and reward media, and creative optimisation. On the business front, the company aims to deepen relationships with large Indian enterprises and build a scalable mid-market business through sector-focused solutions and strategic agency partnerships. As part of its long-term growth plans, the company said it will continue strengthening its leadership pipeline through strategic hiring and future-ready talent initiatives. It also plans to expand its ESG efforts, which currently include planting 15,000 trees and supporting community healthcare programmes. Commenting on the results, Ishank Joshi, Founder, MD & CEO, Mobavenue AI, said: "FY2026 has been a milestone year for Mobavenue AI Tech Limited, defined by disciplined growth, strong execution and meaningful progress in our AI-led transformation. We delivered FY2026 revenue of Rs. 218.48 Cr and EBITDA of Rs. 45.37 Cr at a margin of 20.8%, alongside a strong Q4 performance with revenue growth of 41.9% YoY and EBITDA growth of 67.5% YoY, supported by strong demand from direct advertisers, agency partnerships and international operations. During the year, we expanded our presence across the UK and LATAM regions, completed the integration of Mobavenue Media Private Limited and advanced our proprietary AI stack, which processes 125+ crore signals daily with decisioning in under 15 milliseconds, reaching 2.5 billion devices worldwide. This is a structural advantage that powers measurable outcomes for our clients. As we enter FY2027, our focus is clear: scale with discipline, deepen AI and data-led innovation and expand our global market presence as part of Mission 2030 to build a global AI-native platform from India for the world."

title: Canva loses senior leaders amid push into artificial intelligence
description: Four people in senior roles at Australia’s largest technology company will leave this month amid broader staff concerns about job security.

title: AI ROI Is Bigger Than Efficiency
description: AI has entered a more demanding phase in the enterprise. Enthusiasm and experimentation alone isn’t enough anymore; leaders must show measurable returns. AlphaSense data reflects that shift, with mentions of AI ROI in earnings transcripts up 116% year over year and references to successful AI deployments rising 83%. Even major players are making hard choices [...] The post AI ROI Is Bigger Than Efficiency appeared first on AiThority .

title: Reasons Why Popular AI Is Suddenly And Unexpectedly Telling People To Consider Sleeping Or Getting Some Rest
description: Anthropic Claude is telling people to get sleep or rest, even though the person did not bring up that topic. Why is AI doing this? An AI Insider analysis and scoop.
